commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal code-mode namespace registry, QuickJS namespace bridge, tests, docs, and plugin rollback cleanup without adding a public plugin SDK namespace API.

PR surface: Source +535, Tests +464, Docs +44. Total +1043 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds an internal code-mode namespace capability rather than reporting a reproducible bug. The review inspected the source and test coverage instead of running tests because the task is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer label plus the new code-mode host bridge require human maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat: add internal code mode namespaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge so host-owned namespace globals can be exposed inside `exec` without adding model-visible tools.
- Gate namespaces by run catalog ownership, serialize only safe enumerable scope values, reject forged/unsafe paths, preserve namespace runtime across `wait`, and clear namespace registrations on plugin rollback.
- Document the internal namespace boundary and explicitly keep a public plugin SDK namespace API out of this PR until it can be loader-owned and cache-safe.

## Verification

- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (44 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=0 fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:changed`
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: code mode can expose internal host-owned namespace globals inside QuickJS while keeping normal tools hidden behind `exec` and `wait`.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with Node 24.15.0; local changed gate used after Blacksmith Testbox bootstrap runs failed before meaningful checks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted code-mode Vitest, plugin SDK API baseline test, plugin SDK API check, local changed gate, full build, and final autoreview as listed above.

Evidence after fix: code-mode test file passed 44/44; changed gate passed conflict guards, dependency guards, typecheck, lint, sidecar loader guard, import cycles, and related core guards; build passed; autoreview reported the patch correct.

Observed result after fix: namespace globals are visible only when the owning plugin id has the required catalog tools in the run, same-named tools from other owners do not expose the namespace, forged namespace paths are rejected, and namespace calls survive suspend/resume.

What was not tested: no real third-party plugin namespace integration, because this PR intentionally does not add a public plugin SDK namespace API.
target.author: steipete
